summary: The IP initiated an automated reconnaissance scan, characterized by an initial web probe using the CensysInspect user-agent, followed by an attempt to upgrade to WebSocket and sending raw, non-HTTP data, suggesting broader port and service enumeration.
The IP performed a basic HTTP GET request to the root, likely probing for an active web server.
The IP sent a non-standard string to the honeypot, indicating an attempt to probe for specific application layer services or protocols.
The IP, identified by the zgrab user-agent, specifically probed for the cachemgr.cgi path, indicating a scan for exposed Squid proxy manager interfaces.
This IP executed a standard HTTP GET request to the root, consistent with a generic web server reconnaissance scan.
The IP initiated a connection and then performed a GET request to the root using a Go-http-client user-agent, typical of automated web reconnaissance.
The IP performed a GET request for '/startpage/start.html', characteristic of an automated web scanner probing for common entry points or default web pages.
This IP initiated a basic HTTP GET request with a generic Go-http-client user agent, indicative of a general web scanner probing for services.
This IP made multiple connections without sending clear HTTP requests, indicating a port scan or idle connections from a scanner.
This IP conducted extensive and sophisticated vulnerability scanning and exploit attempts against common web application vulnerabilities.
This IP performed a basic HTTP GET request with a user agent identifying itself as 'ivre-masscan', indicating broad port scanning activity.
This IP probed a specific path (`/SDK/webLanguage`), indicative of scanning for IoT devices or embedded systems known to have vulnerabilities.
This IP made multiple connections without sending clear HTTP requests, indicating a port scan or idle connections from a scanner.
This IP attempted a CGI-BIN path traversal attack to achieve remote code execution, trying to download and execute a malicious shell script.
This IP performed scanning activity while identifying itself as a legitimate security research or asset discovery service (Palo Alto Networks Cortex Xpanse).
This IP attempted to log into an administrative form with generic credentials, indicative of a credential stuffing or brute-force attack.

This IP aggressively scanned for various sensitive configuration and source code files, aiming to exploit misconfigurations.
